nextToken.description: The output displays the token for the next set of application results. This is required for pagination and is available as a response of the previous request.

releaseLabel.description: The Amazon EMR release associated with the application.

type.description: The type of application, such as Spark or Hive.

createdAt.description: The date and time when the application was created.

updatedAt.description: The date and time when the application was last updated.

applicationId.description: The output contains the application ID on which the job run is cancelled.

jobRunId.description: The output contains the ID of the cancelled job run.

name.description: The output contains the name of the application.

arn.description: The output contains the ARN of the application.

application.description: The output displays information about the specified application.

releaseLabel.description: The Amazon EMR release associated with the application.

type.description: The type of application, such as Spark or Hive.

maximumCapacity.description: The maximum capacity of the application. This is cumulative across all workers at any given point in time during the lifespan of the application is created. No new resources will be created once any one of the defined limits is hit.

cpu.description: The maximum allowed CPU for an application.

memory.description: The maximum allowed resources for an application.

disk.description: The maximum allowed disk for an application.

createdAt.description: The date and time when the application run was created.

updatedAt.description: The date and time when the application run was last updated.

autoStartConfiguration.description: The configuration for an application to automatically start on job submission.

enabled.description: Enables the application to automatically start on job submission. Defaults to true.

autoStopConfiguration.description: The configuration for an application to automatically stop after a certain amount of time being idle.

enabled.description: Enables the application to automatically stop after a certain amount of time being idle. Defaults to true.

idleTimeoutMinutes.description: The amount of idle time in minutes after which your application will automatically stop. Defaults to 15 minutes.

networkConfiguration.description: The network configuration for customer VPC connectivity for the application.

subnetIds.description: The array of subnet Ids for customer VPC connectivity.

securityGroupIds.description: The array of security group Ids for customer VPC connectivity.

imageConfiguration.description: The image configuration applied to all worker types.

resolvedImageDigest.description: The SHA256 digest of the image URI. This indicates which specific image the application is configured for. The image digest doesn't exist until an application has started.

workerTypeSpecifications.description: The specification applied to each worker type.

runtimeConfiguration.description: The Configuration specifications of an application. Each configuration consists of a classification and properties. You use this parameter when creating or updating an application. To see the runtimeConfiguration object of an application, run the GetApplication API operation.

s3MonitoringConfiguration.description: The Amazon S3 configuration for monitoring log publishing.

logUri.description: The Amazon S3 destination URI for log publishing.

encryptionKeyArn.description: The KMS key ARN to encrypt the logs published to the given Amazon S3 destination.

managedPersistenceMonitoringConfiguratio.description: The managed log persistence configuration for a job run.

enabled.description: Enables managed logging and defaults to true. If set to false, managed logging will be turned off.

encryptionKeyArn.description: The KMS key ARN to encrypt the logs stored in managed log persistence.

cloudWatchLoggingConfiguration.description: The Amazon CloudWatch configuration for monitoring logs. You can configure your jobs to send log information to CloudWatch.

logGroupName.description: The name of the log group in Amazon CloudWatch Logs where you want to publish your logs.

logStreamNamePrefix.description: Prefix for the CloudWatch log stream name.

encryptionKeyArn.description: The Key Management Service (KMS) key ARN to encrypt the logs that you store in CloudWatch Logs.

logTypes.description:
The types of logs that you want to publish to CloudWatch. If you don't specify any log types, driver STDOUT and STDERR logs will be published to CloudWatch Logs by default. For more information including the supported worker types for Hive and Spark, see Logging for EMR Serverless with CloudWatch.

  • Key Valid Values: SPARK_DRIVER, SPARK_EXECUTOR, HIVE_DRIVER, TEZ_TASK

  • Array Members Valid Values: STDOUT, STDERR, HIVE_LOG, TEZ_AM, SYSTEM_LOGS

prometheusMonitoringConfiguration.description: The monitoring configuration object you can configure to send metrics to Amazon Managed Service for Prometheus for a job run.

remoteWriteUrl.description: The remote write URL in the Amazon Managed Service for Prometheus workspace to send metrics to.

interactiveConfiguration.description: The interactive configuration object that enables the interactive use cases for an application.

studioEnabled.description: Enables you to connect an application to Amazon EMR Studio to run interactive workloads in a notebook.

livyEndpointEnabled.description: Enables an Apache Livy endpoint that you can connect to and run interactive jobs.

schedulerConfiguration.description: The scheduler configuration for batch and streaming jobs running on this application. Supported with release labels emr-7.0.0 and above.

queueTimeoutMinutes.description: The maximum duration in minutes for the job in QUEUED state. If scheduler configuration is enabled on your application, the default value is 360 minutes (6 hours). The valid range is from 15 to 720.

maxConcurrentRuns.description: The maximum concurrent job runs on this application. If scheduler configuration is enabled on your application, the default value is 15. The valid range is 1 to 1000.

identityCenterConfiguration.description: The IAM Identity Center configuration applied to enable trusted identity propagation.

identityCenterInstanceArn.description: The ARN of the IAM Identity Center instance.

identityCenterApplicationArn.description: The ARN of the EMR Serverless created IAM Identity Center Application that provides trusted-identity propagation.

userBackgroundSessionsEnabled.description: Enables user background sessions for this application so Livy sessions can continue running after users log out of their interactive notebook or their Identity Center sessions expire.

url.description: The URL to view job run's dashboard.

jobRun.description: The output displays information about the job run.

applicationId.description: The ID of the application the job is running on.

name.description: The optional job run name. This doesn't have to be unique.

arn.description: The execution role ARN of the job run.

createdBy.description: The user who created the job run.

createdAt.description: The date and time when the job run was created.

updatedAt.description: The date and time when the job run was updated.

executionRole.description: The execution role ARN of the job run.

executionIamPolicy.description: Optional IAM policy. The resulting job IAM role permissions will be an intersection of the policies passed and the policy associated with your job execution role.

policy.description: An IAM inline policy to use as an execution IAM policy.

policyArns.description: A list of Amazon Resource Names (ARNs) to use as an execution IAM policy.

stateDetails.description: The state details of the job run.

releaseLabel.description: The Amazon EMR release associated with the application your job is running on.

configurationOverrides.description: The configuration settings that are used to override default configuration.

logUri.description: The Amazon S3 destination URI for log publishing.

encryptionKeyArn.description: The KMS key ARN to encrypt the logs published to the given Amazon S3 destination.

managedPersistenceMonitoringConfiguratio.description: The managed log persistence configuration for a job run.

enabled.description: Enables managed logging and defaults to true. If set to false, managed logging will be turned off.

encryptionKeyArn.description: The KMS key ARN to encrypt the logs stored in managed log persistence.

logGroupName.description: The name of the log group in Amazon CloudWatch Logs where you want to publish your logs.

logStreamNamePrefix.description: Prefix for the CloudWatch log stream name.

encryptionKeyArn.description: The Key Management Service (KMS) key ARN to encrypt the logs that you store in CloudWatch Logs.

logTypes.description:
The types of logs that you want to publish to CloudWatch. If you don't specify any log types, driver STDOUT and STDERR logs will be published to CloudWatch Logs by default. For more information including the supported worker types for Hive and Spark, see Logging for EMR Serverless with CloudWatch.

remoteWriteUrl.description: The remote write URL in the Amazon Managed Service for Prometheus workspace to send metrics to.

jobDriver.description: The job driver for the job run.

sparkSubmit.description: The job driver parameters specified for Spark.

entryPoint.description: The entry point for the Spark submit job run.

entryPointArguments.description: The arguments for the Spark submit job run.

sparkSubmitParameters.description: The parameters for the Spark submit job run.

hive.description: The job driver parameters specified for Hive.

query.description: The query for the Hive job run.

initQueryFile.description: The query file for the Hive job run.

parameters.description: The parameters for the Hive job run.

tags.description: The tags assigned to the job run.

totalResourceUtilization.description: The aggregate vCPU, memory, and storage resources used from the time the job starts to execute, until the time the job terminates, rounded up to the nearest second.

vCPUHour.description: The aggregated vCPU used per hour from the time job start executing till the time job is terminated.

memoryGBHour.description: The aggregated memory used per hour from the time job start executing till the time job is terminated.

storageGBHour.description: The aggregated storage used per hour from the time job start executing till the time job is terminated.

networkConfiguration.description: The network configuration for customer VPC connectivity.

subnetIds.description: The array of subnet Ids for customer VPC connectivity.

securityGroupIds.description: The array of security group Ids for customer VPC connectivity.

totalExecutionDurationSeconds.description: The job run total execution duration in seconds. This field is only available for job runs in a COMPLETED, FAILED, or CANCELLED state.

executionTimeoutMinutes.description: Returns the job run timeout value from the StartJobRun call. If no timeout was specified, then it returns the default timeout of 720 minutes.

billedResourceUtilization.description: The aggregate vCPU, memory, and storage that Amazon Web Services has billed for the job run. The billed resources include a 1-minute minimum usage for workers, plus additional storage over 20 GB per worker. Note that billed resources do not include usage for idle pre-initialized workers.

vCPUHour.description: The aggregated vCPU used per hour from the time the job starts executing until the job is terminated.

memoryGBHour.description: The aggregated memory used per hour from the time the job starts executing until the job is terminated.

storageGBHour.description: The aggregated storage used per hour from the time the job starts executing until the job is terminated.

retryPolicy.description: The retry policy of the job run.

maxAttempts.description: Maximum number of attempts for the job run. This parameter is only applicable for BATCH mode.

maxFailedAttemptsPerHour.description: Maximum number of failed attempts per hour. This [arameter is only applicable for STREAMING mode.

attemptCreatedAt.description: The date and time of when the job run attempt was created.

attemptUpdatedAt.description: The date and time of when the job run attempt was last updated.

startedAt.description: The date and time when the job moved to the RUNNING state.

endedAt.description: The date and time when the job was terminated.

queuedDurationMilliseconds.description: The total time for a job in the QUEUED state in milliseconds.

jobRunAttempts.description: The array of the listed job run attempt objects.

nextToken.description: The output displays the token for the next set of application results. This is required for pagination and is available as a response of the previous request.

updatedAt.description: The date and time of when the job run attempt was last updated.

state.description: The state of the job run attempt.
